_comment: ClawBio Skill Spec Template — copy this file, fill in every field, then run:
name: my-skill-name
_name_comment: REQUIRED. Lowercase letters, digits, hyphens only. e.g. pharmgx-reporter, variant-annotator
description: One-sentence description of what this skill does for the user.
_description_comment: REQUIRED. Keep it under 120 characters. Start with a verb (Analyse, Predict, Generate…).
author: Your Name
_author_comment: REQUIRED. Your name or GitHub handle.
_version_comment: Semantic version. Start at 0.1.0 for an MVP.
_license_comment: Must match the ClawBio repo license (MIT).
_domain_comment: Primary scientific domain. Examples: genomics, metagenomics, proteomics, pharmacogenomics, transcriptomics, epigenomics, bioinformatics.
_emoji_comment: Single emoji that represents your skill. Shown in logs and reports.
_cli_alias_comment: Short alias for `python clawbio.py run <alias>`. Lowercase, no hyphens. e.g. pharmgx, varann, myskill.
_tags_comment: Free-form list of tags for catalog search. Include domain, data type, and use case.
_trigger_keywords_comment: Natural-language phrases that should route the user here. The orchestrator matches these against user queries. Include synonyms and common misspellings.
capabilities: Parse VCF/TSV input and validate required columns
Generate a clinical-style markdown report with tier classifications
Produce a machine-readable result.json and reproducibility bundle
_capabilities_comment: List 3-6 concrete, testable capabilities. Each maps to a bullet point in SKILL.md Core Capabilities.
_input_formats_comment: One entry per accepted input format. 'required_fields' is a comma-separated string of mandatory column/field names.

dependencies._comment: List pip package specs (e.g. 'biopython>=1.81'). Keep required[] minimal — prefer stdlib. Optional packages must degrade gracefully.

_chaining_partners_comment: Skill names (not aliases) that this skill commonly chains with. Used to generate the Integration section of SKILL.md.
